title: India Poised to Emerge as Global Hub for Green Hydrogen Innovation: Union Minister of State Shri Shripad Yesso Naik
content:
India is positioning itself to become a global innovation hub in green hydrogen, said Union Minister of State Shri Shripad Yesso Naik at the closing session of the first Green Hydrogen R&D Conference, organised by the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) under the National Green Hydrogen Mission from 11-12 September 2025 in New Delhi.
He emphasized that research must go beyond theory into pilots, prototypes, and commercial deployment to ensure green hydrogen becomes cost-competitive and widely accessible. Startups and young researchers were encouraged to think disruptively, integrating academia, industry, and entrepreneurship.
Green hydrogen is expected to play a major role in decarbonising sectors such as steel, cement, fertilizer, mobility, and shipping. It will also help reduce Indiaâ€™s dependence on energy imports, create high-value jobs, and strengthen industrial competitiveness.
At the conference, over 1,300 participants took part in 17 technical sessions, five panel discussions, eight roundtables, and a startup expo showcasing innovations in hydrogen technologies. The National Green Hydrogen Mission has already published over 140 standards to help guide and scale the sector, underscoring Indiaâ€™s commitment to building a robust ecosystem for green hydrogen research, innovation, and deployment.
author: Analysts Account
